words.definitionEn: to agree, often unwillingly, that something is true
words.exampleEn: It was a stupid thing to do, I admit.

words.definitionEn: used to describe a situation in which people or organizations compete against each other
words.exampleEn: competitive games/sports

words.definitionEn: a person who takes part in a competition
words.exampleEn: Over 200 competitors entered the race.

words.definitionEn: a reason for not being satisfied; a statement that somebody makes saying that they are not satisfied
words.exampleEn: a formal complaint

words.definitionEn: made of many different things or parts that are connected; difficult to understand
words.exampleEn: a complex problem/issue/process/system

words.definitionEn: to give all your attention to something and not think about anything else
words.exampleEn: I can't concentrate with all that noise going on.

words.definitionEn: to decide or believe something as a result of what you have heard or seen
words.exampleEn: conclude something, It is too early for us to conclude anything.

words.definitionEn: something that you decide when you have thought about all the information connected with the situation
words.exampleEn: We can safely draw some conclusions from our discussion.

words.definitionEn: feeling sure about your own ability to do things and be successful
words.exampleEn: She was in a relaxed, confident mood.

words.definitionEn: to state or show that something is definitely true or correct, especially by providing evidence
words.exampleEn: confirm something, His guilty expression confirmed my suspicions.

words.definitionEn: to make somebody unable to think clearly or understand something
words.exampleEn: confuse somebody, These two sets of statistics are guaranteed to confuse the public.

words.definitionEn: unable to think clearly or to understand what is happening or what somebody is saying
words.exampleEn: I'm confused—say all that again.

words.definitionEn: something that connects two facts, ideas, etc.
words.exampleEn: connection between A and B, Scientists have established a connection between cholesterol levels and heart disease.

words.definitionEn: a result of something that has happened, especially an unpleasant result
words.exampleEn: Remember that actions have consequences.

words.definitionEn: having the most modern and recently developed ideas, methods, etc.
words.exampleEn: Scientists are working on highly advanced technology to replace fossil fuels.

words.definitionEn: to use something, especially fuel, energy or time
words.exampleEn: The electricity industry consumes large amounts of fossil fuels.

words.definitionEn: a person who buys goods or uses services
words.exampleEn: consumer spending/demand

words.definitionEn: the act of communicating with somebody, especially regularly
words.exampleEn: contact with somebody, I don't have much contact with my uncle.

words.definitionEn: to communicate with somebody, for example by phone, letter or email
words.exampleEn: I've been trying to contact you all day.

words.definitionEn: a box, bottle, etc. in which something can be stored or transported
words.exampleEn: a plastic container

words.definitionEn: the things that are contained in something
words.exampleEn: He tipped the contents of the bag onto the table.

words.definitionEn: happening or existing for a period of time without being interrupted
words.exampleEn: Recovery after the accident will be a continuous process that may take several months.

words.definitionEn: a difference between two or more people or things that you can see clearly when they are compared or put close together; the fact of comparing two or more things in order to show the differences between them
words.exampleEn: The two cities make an interesting contrast.

words.definitionEn: to compare two things in order to show the differences between them
words.exampleEn: contrast A and B, The poem contrasts youth and age.

words.definitionEn: useful, easy or quick to do; not causing problems
words.exampleEn: Fruit is a convenient source of vitamins and energy.

words.definitionEn: to make somebody/yourself believe that something is true
words.exampleEn: convince somebody/yourself, Are the prime minister's assurances enough to convince the public?

words.definitionEn: to become cool or cooler; to make somebody/something become cool or cooler
words.exampleEn: Glass contracts as it cools.

words.definitionEn: to tell somebody what you think they should do in a particular situation
words.exampleEn: advise against doing something, I would strongly advise against going out on your own.

words.definitionEn: the clothes worn by people from a particular place or during a particular historical period
words.exampleEn: an exhibition of Victorian costumes

words.definitionEn: a small house, especially in the country
words.exampleEn: a thatched cottage

words.definitionEn: a plant grown in warm countries for the soft white hairs around its seeds that are used to make cloth and thread
words.exampleEn: From the age of six she worked in the fields, picking cotton.

words.definitionEn: an act of counting to find the total number of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: If the election result is close, there will be a second count.

words.definitionEn: land outside towns and cities, with fields, woods, etc.
words.exampleEn: The surrounding countryside is windswept and rocky.

words.definitionEn: the place where legal trials take place and where crimes, etc. are judged
words.exampleEn: the civil/criminal courts

words.definitionEn: a thing that is put over or on another thing, usually to protect it or to decorate it
words.exampleEn: Brighten up your room with some colourful cushion covers.

words.definitionEn: having a layer or amount of something on it
words.exampleEn: His face was covered in blood.

words.definitionEn: between yellow and white in colour
words.exampleEn: a cream linen suit

words.definitionEn: to have enough money to be able to buy or do something
words.exampleEn: afford something, Can we afford a new car?

words.definitionEn: connected with or involving crime
words.exampleEn: criminal offences/activities

words.definitionEn: having a desire to cause physical or mental pain and make somebody suffer
words.exampleEn: He was known to be a cruel dictator.

words.definitionEn: connected with the culture of a particular society or group, its customs, beliefs, etc.
words.exampleEn: Teachers need to be aware of cultural differences.

words.definitionEn: the system of money that a country uses
words.exampleEn: trading in foreign currencies

words.definitionEn: happening now; of the present time
words.exampleEn: The necklace would be worth over $5 000 at current prices.

words.definitionEn: at the present time
words.exampleEn: The hourly charge is currently £35.

words.definitionEn: a piece of cloth that is hung to cover a window
words.exampleEn: to draw/pull/close the curtains (= to pull them across the window so they cover it)

words.definitionEn: an accepted way of behaving or of doing things in a society or a community
words.exampleEn: It's a local custom.

words.definitionEn: a wound caused by something sharp
words.exampleEn: cuts and bruises on the face

words.definitionEn: every day
words.exampleEn: The machines are inspected twice daily.

words.definitionEn: physical harm caused to something which makes it less attractive, useful or valuable
words.exampleEn: serious/severe damage

words.definitionEn: to have a bad or harmful effect on something/somebody
words.exampleEn: The church was badly damaged by the 1997 earthquake.

words.definitionEn: an agreement, especially in business, on particular conditions for buying or doing something
words.exampleEn: to sign/strike/finalize/close a deal

words.definitionEn: a period of ten years, especially a continuous period, such as 1910–1919 or 2000–2009
words.exampleEn: the early decades of the nineteenth century

words.definitionEn: to become older
words.exampleEn: As he aged, his memory got worse.

words.definitionEn: to make something look more attractive by putting things on it
words.exampleEn: decorate something with something, They decorated the room with flowers and balloons.

words.definitionEn: a long way below the surface of something or a long way inside or into something
words.exampleEn: Dig deeper!

words.definitionEn: of the age of
words.exampleEn: They have two children aged six and nine.

words.definitionEn: to say or explain what the meaning of a word or phrase is
words.exampleEn: define something, The term ‘normal’ is difficult to define.

words.definitionEn: sure or certain; unlikely to change
words.exampleEn: Can you give me a definite answer by tomorrow?

words.definitionEn: an explanation of the meaning of a word or phrase, especially in a dictionary
words.exampleEn: The dictionary provides clear, simple definitions.

words.definitionEn: to take goods, letters, etc. to the person or people they have been sent to
words.exampleEn: deliver something, I get my food delivered from the supermarket to save time.

words.definitionEn: used to emphasize that something is completely true
words.exampleEn: You're absolutely right.

words.definitionEn: a person whose job is to act for, or manage the affairs of, other people in business, politics, etc.
words.exampleEn: Our agent in New York deals with all US sales.

words.definitionEn: an act of leaving a place
words.exampleEn: His sudden departure threw the office into chaos.

words.definitionEn: an arrangement, a promise or a contract made with somebody
words.exampleEn: an international peace agreement

words.definitionEn: used to show that something happened or is true although something else might have happened to prevent it
words.exampleEn: Her voice was shaking despite all her efforts to control it.

words.definitionEn: a place to which somebody/something is going or being sent
words.exampleEn: popular tourist/holiday destinations like the Bahamas

words.definitionEn: to discover the facts about something; to calculate something exactly
words.exampleEn: determine something, An inquiry was set up to determine the cause of the accident.

words.definitionEn: having made a definite decision to do something and not letting anyone prevent you
words.exampleEn: The opposition to her plan made her more determined than ever.

words.definitionEn: the steady growth of something so that it becomes more advanced, stronger, etc.
words.exampleEn: a baby’s development in the womb

words.definitionEn: a simple drawing using lines to explain where something is, how something works, etc.
words.exampleEn: The results are shown in diagram 2.

words.definitionEn: a clear precious stone of pure carbon, the hardest substance known. Diamonds are used in jewellery and also in industry, especially for cutting glass.
words.exampleEn: a diamond ring/necklace

words.definitionEn: further forward in space or time; in front
words.exampleEn: I'll run ahead and warn them.

words.definitionEn: a problem; a thing or situation that causes problems
words.exampleEn: the difficulties of English syntax

words.definitionEn: without stopping or changing direction or vehicle
words.exampleEn: direct to something, We flew direct to Hong Kong.

words.definitionEn: to control or be in charge of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: He was asked to take command and direct operations.

words.definitionEn: without stopping or changing direction
words.exampleEn: The path leads directly to the river.

words.definitionEn: any substance that makes something dirty, for example dust, soil or mud
words.exampleEn: His clothes were covered in dirt.

words.definitionEn: something that causes problems and tends to stop somebody/something from succeeding or making progress
words.exampleEn: a serious/severe/significant disadvantage

words.definitionEn: upset because something you hoped for has not happened or been as good, successful, etc. as you expected
words.exampleEn: The singer has promised to refund any disappointed fans.

words.definitionEn: not as good, successful, etc. as you had hoped; making you feel disappointed
words.exampleEn: a disappointing result/performance/defeat

words.definitionEn: an amount of money that is taken off the usual cost of something
words.exampleEn: to get/offer a discount

words.definitionEn: the purpose of doing something; what somebody is trying to achieve
words.exampleEn: the stated aims of the study

words.definitionEn: a feeling of not liking somebody/something
words.exampleEn: dislike of somebody/something, He did not try to hide his dislike of his boss.

words.definitionEn: to not like somebody/something
words.exampleEn: dislike somebody/something, Why do you dislike him so much?

words.definitionEn: to try or plan to achieve something
words.exampleEn: He has always aimed high (= tried to achieve a lot).

words.definitionEn: to separate into parts; to make something separate into parts
words.exampleEn: The cells began to divide rapidly.

words.definitionEn: a film or a radio or television programme giving facts about something
words.exampleEn: There were some interesting interviews in the documentary.

words.definitionEn: a loud noise or a signal that warns people of danger or of a problem
words.exampleEn: She decided to sound the alarm (= warn people that the situation was dangerous).

words.definitionEn: to give money, food, clothes, etc. to somebody/something, especially a charity
words.exampleEn: donate something to somebody/something, He donated thousands of pounds to charity.

words.definitionEn: in twos or in two parts
words.exampleEn: I thought I was seeing double (= seeing two of something).

words.definitionEn: a feeling of being uncertain about something or not believing something
words.exampleEn: a feeling of doubt and uncertainty

words.definitionEn: to feel uncertain about something; to feel that something is not true, will probably not happen, etc.
words.exampleEn: doubt something, There seems no reason to doubt her story.

words.definitionEn: a book in which you keep photographs, stamps, etc.
words.exampleEn: a photo album

words.definitionEn: wearing clothes and not naked or wearing clothes for sleeping
words.exampleEn: Hurry up and get dressed.

words.definitionEn: drinks such as beer, wine, etc. that can make people drunk
words.exampleEn: He never drinks alcohol.

words.definitionEn: a very small amount of liquid that forms a round shape
words.exampleEn: The first drops of rain fell.

words.definitionEn: connected with or containing alcohol
words.exampleEn: alcoholic drinks/beverages

words.definitionEn: a musical instrument made of a hollow round frame with plastic or skin stretched tightly across one or both ends. You play it by hitting it with sticks or with your hands.
words.exampleEn: to play the drum

words.definitionEn: having drunk so much alcohol that it is impossible to think or speak clearly
words.exampleEn: They were clearly too drunk to drive.

words.definitionEn: caused by somebody/something; because of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: The team's success was largely due to her efforts.

words.definitionEn: a fine powder that consists of very small pieces of sand, earth, etc.
words.exampleEn: A cloud of dust rose as the truck drove off.

words.definitionEn: something that you feel you have to do because it is your moral or legal responsibility
words.exampleEn: duty to do something, It is my duty to report it to the police.

words.definitionEn: a sudden, violent shaking of the earth’s surface
words.exampleEn: a devastating/massive/powerful earthquake

words.definitionEn: located in the east or facing east
words.exampleEn: eastern Spain

words.definitionEn: connected with the trade, industry and development of wealth of a country, an area or a society
words.exampleEn: They discussed social, economic and political issues.

words.definitionEn: the relationship between production, trade and the supply of money in a particular country or region
words.exampleEn: The economy is in recession.

words.definitionEn: the outside limit of an object, a surface or an area; the part furthest from the centre
words.exampleEn: I gripped the edge of my desk to steady myself.

words.definitionEn: a person who is in charge of a newspaper, magazine, etc., or part of one, and who decides what should be included
words.exampleEn: the editor of the Washington Post

words.definitionEn: to teach somebody over a period of time at a school, university, etc.
words.exampleEn: be educated, She was educated in the US.

words.definitionEn: having had the kind of education mentioned; having been to the school, college or university mentioned
words.exampleEn: privately educated children

words.definitionEn: connected with education
words.exampleEn: children with special educational needs

words.definitionEn: producing the result that is wanted or intended; producing a successful result
words.exampleEn: Aspirin is a simple but highly effective treatment.

words.definitionEn: in a way that produces the intended result or a successful result
words.exampleEn: The company must reduce costs to compete effectively.

words.definitionEn: an attempt to do something especially when it is difficult to do
words.exampleEn: The project was a team effort.

words.definitionEn: the process of choosing a person or a group of people for a position, especially a political position, by voting
words.exampleEn: presidential/parliamentary/local elections

words.definitionEn: a necessary or typical part of something; one of several parts that something contains
words.exampleEn: There are three important elements to consider.

words.definitionEn: shy, uncomfortable or ashamed, especially in a social situation
words.exampleEn: I've never felt so embarrassed in my life!

words.definitionEn: making you feel shy, uncomfortable or ashamed
words.exampleEn: an embarrassing moment/situation

words.definitionEn: a sudden serious and dangerous event or situation that needs immediate action to deal with it
words.exampleEn: This is a medical emergency needing urgent treatment with antibiotics.

words.definitionEn: a strong feeling such as love, fear or anger; the part of a person’s character that consists of feelings
words.exampleEn: to show/express your emotions

words.definitionEn: work, especially when it is done to earn money; the state of being employed
words.exampleEn: full-time/part-time employment

words.definitionEn: to remove everything that is in a container, etc.
words.exampleEn: empty something, She emptied the bins, washed the glasses and went to bed.

words.definitionEn: to give somebody support, courage or hope
words.exampleEn: encourage somebody, We were greatly encouraged by the positive response of the public.

words.definitionEn: a person who hates somebody or who acts or speaks against somebody/something
words.exampleEn: She didn't have an enemy in the world.

words.definitionEn: having agreed to marry somebody
words.exampleEn: When did you get engaged?

words.definitionEn: the activity of applying scientific knowledge to the design, building and control of machines, roads, bridges, electrical equipment, etc.
words.exampleEn: The bridge is a triumph of modern engineering.

words.definitionEn: to interest somebody or make somebody laugh in order to please them
words.exampleEn: The aim of the series is both to entertain and inform.

words.definitionEn: films, music, etc. used to entertain people; an example of this
words.exampleEn: radio, television and other forms of entertainment

words.definitionEn: a door, gate, passage, etc. used for entering a room, building or place
words.exampleEn: the front/back/side entrance of the house

words.definitionEn: an act of going into or getting into a place
words.exampleEn: She made her entry to the sound of thunderous applause.

words.definitionEn: connected with the natural conditions in which people, animals and plants live; connected with the environment
words.exampleEn: the environmental impact of pollution

words.definitionEn: one part of a story that is broadcast on television or radio in several parts
words.exampleEn: The next episode has not yet been filmed.

words.definitionEn: the same in size, quantity, value, etc. as something else
words.exampleEn: There is an equal number of boys and girls in the class.

words.definitionEn: to be the same in size, quantity, value, etc. as something else
words.exampleEn: 2x plus y equals 7 (= 2x+y=7)
